name: nfdump

documentation.readme: nfdump is a powerful suite of tools for collecting, processing, and analyzing NetFlow, IPFIX, and sFlow data from network devices. It supports advanced filtering, aggregation, and enrichment (geolocation, AS, Tor) of flow data with a focus on efficiency, flexibility, and extensibility.
usage_sections: Features: - Collects NetFlow (v1, v5/v7, v9, IPFIX) and sFlow data. - Converts live or file pcap traffic to flow data. - Multi-threaded for high-performance processing and sorting. - Advanced flow filtering and aggregation (filter syntax similar to tcpdump, but optimized for flow data). -

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

examples: nfdump -r flowfile 'src ip $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST and dst port 443' -A srcip,dstip
nfcapd.YYYYMMDDhhmm (e.g., nfcapd.200907110845 contains data from July 11th 2009 08:45 onward)

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: [![Build Status](https://github.com/phaag/nfdump/actions/workflows/c-cpp.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/phaag/nfdump/actions/workflows/c-cpp.yml).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: nfdump is a powerful suite of tools for collecting, processing, and analyzing NetFlow, IPFIX, and sFlow data from network devices. It supports advanced filtering, aggregation, and enrichment (geolocation, AS, Tor) of flow data with a focus on efficiency, flexibility, and extensibility.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./nfdump -r old-flowfile -y -w new-flowfile`. Further invocations from upstream: `./autogen.sh`; `./configure`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
